attributes.content:
## Overview

The Job Offer card gives the workspace a structured home for employment offer with position, compensation, benefits, start date, and acceptance status.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Job Offer acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Contact]]** -- Job Offer often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Organization]]** -- Job Offer frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Task]]** -- Job Offer often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Job Offer can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Job Offer can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Interview Feedback]]** -- Interview Feedback can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Job Offer.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Job Offer as the structured home for employment offer with position, compensation, benefits, start date, and acceptance status and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Job Offer when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Job Offer stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Job Offer
cardInfo.summary: Employment offer with position, compensation, benefits, start date, and acceptance status.
